ls20 · an AI agent that cleared ARC-AGI-3 (7/7)

game ls20-9607627b7 / 7 levels World-Model-assistedvanilla JS · all data inlined
Every replay below advances one game step per frame — no subsampling. Each pane decodes the recording's 64×64 grid (16-colour palette, RLE-inflated to exactly 4096 cells) and steps through every frame of its segment. (1) Watch the agent solve — a clean L5 beside the final L7 (tried, then won via the World Model). (2) The same-budget recipes-only ablation. (3) The metrics dashboard.
Honesty banner. L7 is the final level; our full-ARA agent solved it on its 2nd attempt via the World Model (the C07-in-fog insight that cracked the chicken-and-egg lock-gate). L5 is a clean solve (one in-game death, then a precise win). The recipes-only same-budget arm operated the controls but plateaued without solving L7. This is a capability / efficiency comparison, not a head-to-head race. All frames, reasoning, and metrics are inlined — the page makes zero external requests.

1 · Watch the agent solve — L5 (clean) beside L7 (try → win)

Two independent replays, each its own scrubber + play/pause + speed. Both step one game step at a time across the full frame set — no subsampling. Left: a clean L5 solve (one death, then a precise win). Right: the final L7, with Attempt 1 and Attempt 2 stitched into one timeline — markers flag the Attempt-1→2 boundary, the WM-consult turning point, and the WIN frame.

2 · Same step budget, recipes-only — the ablation (honest)

A recipes-only agent was given only the L1–L6 success trajectories — no claims, no heuristics, no World Model — and the same total budget our agent used on L7 = 914 steps. Recording: the live_cmp run (7e04b850, L7 segment = 182 frames). Full-frame, one step at a time; reasoning per step from armB_extended_trace.jsonl.

Step budget
ran 181 of 914 steps
Ran 181 of 914 available steps, then plateaued: 174 steps with no new max-progress across 4 RESETs. The budget was never exhausted — it ran out of ideas, not steps.
step 0
trace step 1 / 182 action — Level 7 state NOT_FINISHED panel baseline
recipes-only · brute-forcing the Locksmith loop
recipes-only blind sweep
…
max progress…

Honest result: the recipes-only agent never found L7's lock at all. Its own log flags “control operated” at step 7, but that was a false alarm — a 286-cell fog-of-war viewport shift as the block moved, not the handful of cells a real toggle changes. In the actual frames the lock panel never left baseline; it never aligned the lock, never reached the key-box, and never cleared L7 (levels stayed at 6) — it mistook the fog for progress and plateaued far short. Recipes transfer the family (Locksmith move / lock / deliver), but not L7's specific mechanics — where the controls are, the fog-gated pocket, the lock-gate-in-fog, the word — which live in the World Model that only our agent had.

3 · Metrics dashboard — the whole 7/7 run

Headline cards, per-level token spend, the real-failures vs research-dead-ends split, and the honest steps-vs-dead-ends trend. Every number reads from the inlined metrics.json.

Steps to clear each level (通关步数)

13 → 45 → 49 → 60 → 56 → 120 → 63. The final, hardest level (L7) clears in the fewest steps since L3 — fewer than L6’s 120 — because once the ARA world model cracks the mechanism (via wm-predict), the actual execution is short. The hard part is discovery, not execution; the system pays off where it’s hardest. (L5 56 < L4 60 despite higher complexity: a precompute efficiency win.)

Per-level token spend (first attempt → success)

Real in-game failures (GAME_OVER) vs research dead-ends

Solid = real in-game deaths per level (GAME_OVER). The 12 research dead-ends (falsified hypotheses) are a separate metric, shown as the dashed total — they are epistemic progress, not failures.

Self-contained — vanilla JS + canvas, no external dependencies, no network requests. Double-click to open. Frames RLE-compressed (every frame inflates to exactly 4096 cells, values 0–15). Generated from harness/recordings/, ara-ls20/trace/, and demo-l7/metrics.json.
